title: Malache luschnathiana
uses: Malache luschnathiana has been traditionally used in medicine due to its reported antiseptic and antibacterial properties. This family consists of approximately 130 genera and 2,400 species, many of which are native to the Americas. Malache luschnathiana is an endemic plant found in South America, specifically in Brazil. The classification of Malache luschnathiana within the broader plant kingdom is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, and the clade Eudicots.
